feedback.text: Hello dear members of the Committee of the European Union. I am writing to you today to advance the cause of Stop Killing Games, which is directly linked to the Digital Fairness Regulation. As a customer I am very disturbed by the idea that the digital products I buy online can one day be taken away arbitrarily or for my behaviour and opinion on a platform other than the shop. The very concept of a commercial transaction is at risk because if the seller can withdraw access to the product it does not really belong to me and it is not the terms on which the product was sold to me in the majority of cases. It is sold and not “leased” and these terms are not clearly communicated or obscurciated intentionally by publishers and companies. Recently MasterCard and Visa have recently influenced the giant Steam to remove video games from the bookshop for dubious reasons given that he are only bankers, what customers do privately should not be expected to do. Now see the immense power of a party in the transaction process and that is why I am calling to protect the consumer’s rights to really own the digital products they buy and to limit direct or indirect interference as it inevitably leads to a bad slope where such interference makes things more comfortable elsewhere. Yours sincerely,
